competitors.label: MDN let statement
competitors.selectionReason: MDN's let reference is the canonical docs source for lexical declarations and TDZ behavior, which is central to this comparison.
theirStrengths: It is authoritative on lexical declarations, TDZ, and why let/const differ from var.
theirGaps: It is not a concise side-by-side comparison answer in trivia form.

realWorldUseCases: High-signal answers also mention scope boundaries and redeclaration rules, because that is where production bugs show up.
MDN explains lexical declarations accurately, but our page now ties the comparison more directly to real loop and leakage bugs.
actionableExamples: Its reference details are thorough, while our page now gives a direct loop trap plus a block-leak example.
followUpConfusion: MDN still has wider reference depth, so the broad follow-up comparison stays close.
Add one short note on top-level var in scripts versus modules so the follow-up surface is broader than a let reference.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical let reference, but our page now presents the comparison through loop traps, block leakage, and a single interview-ready takeaway instead of reference-only detail.
